page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is a general-purpose instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 processor using the Neoverse-V2 architecture. It features 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network baseline bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1707.39 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1810.0, both falling in the average category. Single-threaded gzip compression at 30.29 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.13 GB/sec show strong performance. However, multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks are weak, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 3234.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3403.17. Cryptographic performance is poor, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 372.25. Memory bandwidth is mixed, with uncached large-block reads reaching an average of 56.19 GB/sec, while write speeds and small-block cached reads are weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gd.large offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring local NVMe SSD storage alongside moderate ARM64 compute resources. The inclusion of local instance storage reduces the need for external block storage for temporary data, though the low 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth limits high-throughput network operations. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, basic database operations, single-threaded file processing, and lightweight web serving. It is less suitable for multi-threaded applications, heavy cryptography, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with strong single-threaded compression and decompression results. However, multi-core performance, memory write bandwidth, and cryptographic operations are weak. The instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-balanced workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage without requiring high network throughput. It is suited for lightweight database operations, single-threaded file processing, and general-purpose web serving, while being constrained in multi-threaded and network-intensive tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD. It offers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core and cryptographic capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is an ARM64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton processing, balanced memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Includes 8.0 GB of system memory and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers strong single-threaded compression and decompression benchmark results
Exhibits weak multi-core performance and limited 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight database operations, text processing, and general-purpose web serving
